data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "No single critic fixed at one precision/recall operating point can strictly beat self-consistency across a realistic difficulty distribution; the harness strictly wins only when the critic's precision is adaptively boosted on exactly the mid-zone items where the majority is fragile, via question-conditioned (difficulty-adaptive) critic gating that suppresses flags on easy and very-hard items.", "mechanism": "A fixed-operating-point critic faces an inescapable precision-difficulty tradeoff: on easy items (low e) any flag is likely a false flag (precision -> 0, observed v4-flash easy: precision=0.333), and on hard items the critic's own error rate inflates false flags (observed v4-pro hard: precision=0.125). The erosion channel thus dominates at both tails of the difficulty distribution. Only a critic that can condition its flagging threshold on an estimate of pool difficulty—suppressing flags when confidence is low—can concentrate its true-positive flags on the mid-zone where rescue is both possible and needed. The causal mechanism is selection-on-difficulty: adaptive gating shifts the critic's operating point along its precision-recall curve, raising precision in the mid-zone at the cost of recall on tails where recall is worthless anyway.", "scope": {"covers": ["k >= 5 odd", "base error e in (1/(k+1), 1/2) for at least a subset of items", "critic with access to a difficulty signal (logit margin, self-uncertainty, or pool entropy)", "binary QA"]}, "excludes": ["fixed-threshold critic without difficulty conditioning (per refuted 133fcf5d and base-case 55975070)", "e uniformly outside the fragility window", "critic with perfect oracle difficulty estimation (trivial)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Adaptive-gated critic achieves harness accuracy > SC accuracy on mid-zone items but the SAME adaptive-gated critic also beats SC on >= 80% of items outside the fragility window, which would contradict the necessity of the fragility regime", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "No difficulty bin in the experiment shows a statistically significant harness > SC gap (binomial p > 0.05) despite adaptive gating and mid-zone base error, refuting the sufficiency of adaptive precision boosting", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This candidate aligns with 30e483eb's emphasis on question-adaptive critic confidence. It predicts the refuted candidate (133fcf5d) failed precisely because it assumed a fixed critic. The decisive experiment must use a difficulty-stratified design so that each bin has enough items for a binomial test, not just aggregate accuracy. Token budget: ~210k, leaving margin for a replication fold."}
